claim: Materializes the exact first exterior trace normal form P_w=(x_0-x_1)Q_w for the pairwise Schur obstruction, for w=12,16.
claim_boundary: This is not the final Talagrand proof. It certifies the correct first normal-form factor after the naive square-factor route failed.

theorem.interpretation: The obstruction carries exactly one antisymmetric pair trace. The previous square-factor shortcut fails; the correct first normal form is first-difference, not second-difference.
theorem.name: Exterior Trace First-Difference Normal Form
theorem.relation_to_exterior_death: The exterior law kills repeated directions; this certificate identifies the surviving first pair-trace that must be controlled by barrier positivity rather than by naive S_24 symmetry.
theorem.remaining_target: Prove Q_w has the correct sign on the ordered barrier branch x_0>=x_1, B_w>=0, or produce a barrier multiplier/SOS decomposition.
theorem.statement: For w=12,16, the pairwise Schur obstruction polynomial P_w has an exact first exterior trace factor P_w=(x_0-x_1)Q_w.
theorem.verification: The certificate groups P_w by the 22 remaining exponents and verifies pair-total cancellation in x_0,x_1, equivalently zero remainder after division by x_0-x_1.
